source_jobs.title: Don't see a role for you? Feel free to apply directly here!
source_jobs.description: Here at Ready, we are constantly looking for strong talent to up-level our team. If you don't see a role open that directly applies to your skillset, but you're interested in working for Ready.. feel free to apply here! Our recruiting team will reach out when we have a role open similar to what you're looking for.

source_jobs.title: Machinist/Fabrication Lead
source_jobs.description:
Location: San Carlos, CA

We’re building Portals — custom-retrofitted communication pods designed for real-world deployment and shipping at production volume.

We’re looking for a CNC Machinist / Fabrication Lead to own our fabrication cell end-to-end. This role is responsible for programming, setup, machining, and maintaining the equipment used to produce precision enclosures and components machined primarily from aluminum and copper and bronze alloys that house screens, iPads, speakers, cameras, and integrated hardware systems.

This role spans subtractive and additive manufacturing — from CNC milling and laser cutting to metal 3D printing for prototyping and production components. You'll own the full fabrication workflow across all processes.

This is a hands-on production role, not a desk job. You will be running machines daily, improving processes, and ensuring consistent, high-quality output as we scale manufacturing.

If you take pride in holding tight tolerances run after run — and enjoy turning raw material into finished product — you’ll thrive here.

About your role at Ready ⚡️

 - Program, set up, and operate CNC mills (Haas experience preferred) and laser cutting equipment

 - Operate wire-laser directed energy deposition (DED) equipment for additive manufacturing of metal components including custom heat sinks and prototype parts

 - Own fabrication workflows from raw stock to finished enclosure components

 - Design and build fixtures to improve repeatability and throughput

 - Optimize toolpaths, feeds, speeds, and setups for production efficiency

 - Maintain machines, tooling, and fabrication equipment in working order

 - Troubleshoot machining issues and resolve production bottlenecks

 - Perform in-process inspection and ensure parts meet dimensional and cosmetic requirements

 - Collaborate with engineering and assembly teams to improve manufacturability

 - Maintain organized workspaces and uphold shop safety standards

 - Support daily production targets (~2 units/day)

A bit about you 🥇

 - Proven experience programming and operating CNC mills (Haas strongly preferred)

 - Experience operating laser cutters or similar fabrication equipment

 - Develop and refine additive-to-subtractive workflows — printing near-net-shape parts and finish machining to final tolerances

 - Ability to independently manage setup, fixturing, and tooling decisions

 - Strong understanding of machining fundamentals, tolerances, and material behavior

 - Experience in a production or fabrication environment (not only prototyping)

 - Mechanical intuition and strong troubleshooting skills

 - High attention to detail and quality-first mindset

 - Experience performing routine machine maintenance and diagnostics

 - Experience with sheet metal fabrication or enclosure manufacturing

 - Familiarity with Fusion 360, Mastercam, or similar CAM software

 - Experience supporting low-volume production scaling

 - Welding or general fabrication experience

 - Experience working in a startup or fast-moving build environment

Physical Requirements & Work Environment 🏭

The physical requirements described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job with or without reasonable accommodation.

Example:

 - Ability to perform repetitive tasks involving hands, wrists, and arms for extended periods.

 - Ability to stand and/or walk for extended periods during a work shift (up to 8 hours).

 - Ability to lift, carry, push, and pull materials and equipment weighing up to 25 lbs.

 - Ability to bend, stoop, kneel, crouch, and reach as required to perform assembly and inspection tasks.

 - Ability to use hand tools, power tools, and test equipment safely.

 - Visual and auditory ability sufficient to inspect components, read instrumentation, and respond to alarms or verbal instructions.

 - Work may involve exposure to noise, moving mechanical parts, electronics, and va

source_jobs.title: Vice President, Finance
source_jobs.description:
Location: Bay Area, CA

Ready is entering a pivotal phase of growth and is seeking a Vice President of Finance to partner closely with the CEO and executive team to shape our financial strategy as we scale into our next chapter. This leader will build the systems, discipline, and capital strategy necessary to support significant company expansion and future liquidity opportunities.

This role is both strategic and operational. You will establish the financial foundation required to scale a venture-backed SaaS business while preparing the company for the rigor, transparency, and execution required of a public company.

As the VP of Finance, you will be a core architect of Ready’s future — owning capital strategy, investor engagement, financial operations, governance, and IPO execution. You will help translate our ambitious vision into disciplined execution while ensuring we scale responsibly and credibly in the eyes of investors, partners, and regulators.

This role is Bay Area–based, operating in a remote-first environment with frequent travel required for executive collaboration, investor meetings, and key company moments.

About your role at Ready ⚡️

You will serve as both a strategic partner and a hands-on builder, responsible for guiding Ready through hyper-growth and into the public markets.

Key outcomes and responsibilities include:

 - Lead Ready’s financial strategy and long-term capital planning through SPAC IPO execution targeted for 2027

 - Own and maintain the company's capitalization table, equity administration, option plans, and investor ownership records while ensuring accuracy, compliance, and audit readiness

 - Own IPO/SPAC readiness including financial reporting maturity, controls, governance, and public-company infrastructure

 - Partner with the CEO on fundraising, investor relations, and capital markets strategy

 - Build scalable finance, accounting, FP&A, and compliance functions aligned with rapid growth

 - Establish forecasting, KPI frameworks, and operating discipline across the organization

 - Prepare and manage S-1/SPAC documentation, audits, and public reporting requirements

 - Develop relationships with banks, auditors, legal partners, and institutional investors

 - Guide executive decision-making through rigorous financial insight and strategic modeling

 - Support M&A evaluation, strategic partnerships, and long-range corporate planning

 - Help shape Ready’s leadership culture as a trusted executive partner

 - Manage current team of AP/AR, accountants, etc.

This is not a maintenance VP, Finance role — we are looking for a leader excited to build, scale, and take a company public.

A bit about you 🥇

Required experience

 - Proven experience serving as senior finance executive at a venture-backed company

 - Direct experience leading or playing a key leadership role in an IPO or SPAC transaction

 - Deep understanding of capital markets, investor relations, and public company reporting

 - Experience scaling financial operations in a high-growth startup environment

 - Strong operational mindset — comfortable moving between strategy and execution

 - Demonstrated ability to partner closely with founders and executive teams

 - Exceptional communication skills with investors, boards, and cross-functional leaders

 - Based in or willing to relocate to the San Francisco Bay Area

Nice to have

 - Experience working with federal or government markets

 - Background supporting companies selling into regulated or public-sector environments

 - Experience in a hardware or deep-tech company alongside software/SaaS operations

 - Prior SPAC transaction experience

About Ready 🚀

Creative problem solvers approaching a legacy industry with a new point of view
Humble but ambitious, knowledgeable but curious, persistent but not obnoxious
Concise and effective in written and spoken communication
Comfortable working remotely
One team, one dream

About what you get…

 - Competitive salary plus meanin

source_jobs.title: Assembly Technician
source_jobs.description:
Location: San Carlos, CA

We’re building Portals — custom communication pods deployed in libraries and public spaces to expand access to essential digital services. These private, technology-enabled spaces allow people in underserved communities to attend telehealth appointments, take exams, access government services, and connect securely online when they otherwise wouldn’t have a safe or reliable environment.

Every Portal we assemble becomes part of real community infrastructure. Build quality, reliability, and attention to detail directly impact the people relying on these spaces every day.

We are looking for a reliable, detail-oriented Assembly Technician to support the full build cycle.

This is not a single-station production-line job. You’ll work across every stage of assembly — from receiving components to building, wiring, finishing, and preparing units for shipment.

You should be comfortable working with your hands, following structured build processes, and producing consistent, high-quality work at production volume.

About your role at Ready ⚡️

 - Receive and inspect incoming shipments and components

 - Perform incoming quality control checks on parts and materials

 - Assemble enclosure structures and mechanical components

 - Measure, drill, cut, and modify parts for custom fitment

 - Install AV equipment including screens, cameras, speakers, and peripherals

 - Perform wiring, connector installation, and cable terminations

 - Apply PDLC privacy film and other finishing elements requiring precision and care

 - Test assemblies and verify system readiness before shipment

 - Package and crate finished units safely for transport

 - Maintain organized workspaces and follow shop safety procedures

 - Support daily production targets (current goal: ~2 completed units per day)

A bit about you 🥇

 - Experience in AV installation, electromechanical assembly, hardware build, or similar hands-on technical work

 - Proficiency using hand tools, power tools, drills, and cutting equipment

 - Familiarity with wiring connectors, cable routing, and terminations

 - Ability to perform incoming QC and identify defects or inconsistencies

 - Strong attention to detail and pride in clean, precise work

 - Ability to follow documented build procedures consistently

 - Comfort working in a production-paced environment

 - Mechanical aptitude and willingness to learn new processes

 - Builder mindset - you’d rather build it right than fix it twice

Physical Requirements & Work Environment 🏭

The physical requirements described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job with or without reasonable accommodation.

Example:

 - Ability to perform repetitive tasks involving hands, wrists, and arms for extended periods.

 - Ability to stand and/or walk for extended periods during a work shift (up to 8 hours).

 - Ability to lift, carry, push, and pull materials and equipment weighing up to 25 lbs.

 - Ability to bend, stoop, kneel, crouch, and reach as required to perform assembly and inspection tasks.

 - Ability to use hand tools, power tools, and test equipment safely.

 - Visual and auditory ability sufficient to inspect components, read instrumentation, and respond to alarms or verbal instructions.

 - Work may involve exposure to noise, moving mechanical parts, electronics, and varying temperatures typical of a manufacturing or lab environment.

Ready is committed to providing proper personal protective equipment (PPE) and reasonable accommodations to qualified individuals with disabilities in accordance with the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) and applicable state and local laws.

About Ready 🚀

 - Creative problem solvers approaching a legacy industry with a new point of view

 - Humble but ambitious, knowledgeable but curious, persistent but not obnoxious

 - Concise and effective in written and spoken communication

 - One team, one dr

source_jobs.title: Product Solutions Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Location: Austin, TX

Ready is seeking a passionate and innovative Product Solutions Engineer to support our state customers as we rapidly expand. This person will force-multiply our ability to deliver results for key customers and drive expansion into future opportunities. You will translate customer requests into impactful features, navigate development complexities, and bridge the gap between technical feasibility and user-centric design, ensuring our customers' needs don’t fall through the cracks. This person will also contribute to Ready’s Center of Excellence through AI technology and automation.

 

About your role at Ready ⚡️

 - Collaborate with product managers and engineers (PED) to define and prioritize product requirements, ensuring technical feasibility and alignment with user needs.

 - Capture, document, and communicate client requirements with the core team while adhering to the agreed upon format to convey priority.

 - Proactively identify and manage risks and dependencies across cross-functional teams, advocating for solutions and driving efficient execution.

 - Develop, utilize, and perform effective test strategies to ensure quality and performance of implemented features.

 - Document technical specifications and maintain clear communication with stakeholders throughout the development process.

 - Contribute to the continuous improvement of our development processes and tools, promoting a culture of innovation and agility.

 - Stay abreast of emerging technologies and industry trends, proactively suggesting opportunities for product enhancement.

 - Technical Support and Troubleshooting.

 - Client Training and Education on Ready Software tools.

 - Work closely with engineering, product, and operations teams to optimize AI utilization, flag risks/blockers, and drive continuous improvement in workflows.

 

A bit about you 🥇

 - Bachelor's degree with an emphasis on computer science, engineering, or similar technical field.

 - 2-3 years experience working in an engineering role.

 - Strong understanding of Agile development methodologies and experience working in cross-functional teams.

 - Excellent communication, collaboration, and problem-solving skills.

 - Passion for building user-friendly and impactful products.

 - Ability to independently manage multiple tasks and prioritize effectively under pressure.

 - Builder mentality with proven experience in customer facing roles.

 - Ability to write clean, maintainable, and efficient code in at least one relevant programming language (e.g., React, Python etc).

 - Demonstrated understanding of user experience (UX) principles and practices.

 - Strong analytical and data-driven approach to problem-solving.

 - Basic understanding of web technologies such as HTML5, CSS3, and web APIs.

 - Proficiency in AI model monitoring, data validation, and automation tooling.

 - Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, agile environment and manage multiple priorities.

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills.

 - A passion for user-centered design and clean coding practice.

 - Ability to operate independently and thrive in an ambiguous environment.

 - Experience with AWS Bedrock, Python (including SQL knowledge), CI/CD (Docker, AWS), MLflow + Airflow (good to have), and Langchain or Pydantic-AI.

 - Knowledge of LLM model evaluation, RAG architectures, LLM MCP servers, and LLM prompt engineering & function calls.

About Ready 🚀

 - Creative problem solvers approaching a legacy industry with a new point of view

 - Humble but ambitious, knowledgeable but curious, persistent but not obnoxious

 - Concise and effective in written and spoken communication

 - Comfortable working hybrid

 - One team, one dream

About what you get…

 - Competitive salary plus meaningful equity upside; salary range is $125,000-$160,000

 - Competitive (and ever expanding) benefits for employees and dependents

 - Opportunities to learn and grow – all things startups

 - Majori

source_jobs.title: Deployment & Field Operations Lead
source_jobs.description:
Location: San Carlos, CA

We’re building Portals — custom communication pods deployed in libraries and public spaces to expand access to essential services. These private, technology-enabled spaces allow people in underserved communities to attend telehealth appointments, take exams, access government services, and connect securely online when they otherwise wouldn’t have a safe or reliable environment.

Once a Portal leaves our Fab Lab, this role ensures it arrives, installs, and operates successfully in the real world.

We’re looking for a Deployment & Field Operations Lead to own the end-to-end process of delivering Portals from our facility into customer environments.

This role combines hands-on installation work, logistics coordination, and customer implementation. You’ll plan shipments, travel to deployment sites, manage contractors, install systems onsite, and ensure each Portal is operational before handoff.

You’ll act as the bridge between fabrication, logistics, and the customer experience.

This is a builder/operator role, not a desk-only operations job.

About your role at Ready ⚡️

 - Hire and manage local contractors or installation teams when needed

 - Travel to customer locations to receive, unload, assemble, and install Portals onsite

 - Perform final mechanical setup, power-up, and system validation

 - Configure networking, connectivity, and AV systems

 - Troubleshoot issues during deployment and bring systems fully online

 - Train local staff on operation and basic usage

 - Plan shipment logistics from Fab Lab to deployment sites

 - Coordinate freight, delivery timing, and site readiness

 - Organize installation schedules and deployment timelines

 - Ensure installations can be completed within rapid deployment timelines (~1 day installs)

 - Serve as the primary owner of deployment execution

 - Create repeatable installation playbooks and checklists

 - Coordinate with fabrication and assembly teams prior to shipment

 - Track equipment, parts, and deployment readiness

 - Support field troubleshooting and customer support needs post-install

A bit about you 🥇

 - Experience in field installation, AV deployment, hardware implementation, or technical operations

 - Strong mechanical aptitude and comfort assembling physical systems onsite

 - Experience coordinating logistics, vendors, or project execution

 - Ability to troubleshoot hardware, networking, or system issues independently

 - Comfort traveling frequently for installations (40%-60%)

 - Excellent organizational skills and follow-through

 - Confidence interacting directly with customers and site operators

 - AV installation or low-voltage wiring experience

 - Experience managing contractors or installation crews

 - Basic networking or WiFi configuration experience

 - Experience deploying hardware products in the field

 - Background in facilities, construction coordination, or technical operations

Physical Requirements & Work Environment 🏭

The physical requirements described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job with or without reasonable accommodation.

Example:

 - Ability to perform repetitive tasks involving hands, wrists, and arms for extended periods.

 - Ability to stand and/or walk for extended periods during a work shift (up to 8 hours).

 - Ability to lift, carry, push, and pull materials and equipment weighing up to 25 lbs.

 - Ability to bend, stoop, kneel, crouch, and reach as required to perform assembly and inspection tasks.

 - Ability to use hand tools, power tools, and test equipment safely.

 - Visual and auditory ability sufficient to inspect components, read instrumentation, and respond to alarms or verbal instructions.

 - Work may involve exposure to noise, moving mechanical parts, electronics, and varying temperatures typical of a manufacturing or lab environment.

Ready is committed to providing proper personal protecti

source_jobs.title: Operations & Logistics Manager
source_jobs.description:
Location: Fab Lab – San Carlos, CA

Ready is a remote-first Public Benefit Corporation building the Utility Intelligence Platform that helps governments, utility operators, and consultants manage complex infrastructure programs end-to-end.

As Ready expands its hardware product lines (Hypercube, our field-deployable infrastructure platform, and Portals, our communications hubs), physical operations have become mission-critical. The Fab Lab in San Carlos is the center of our hardware development, testing, assembly, staging, and deployment efforts.

We're looking for an Operations & Logistics Manager to own the operational backbone of the Fab Lab: the systems that move hardware from production to deployment and keep the space running so Ready can continue building. This is a hands-on role for someone who solves problems from first principles and builds the processes that make routine work easy and automatic, all while nurturing a space where creativity and genius can flourish.

This is an onsite role based in San Carlos, CA, five days per week.

About Your Role at Ready ⚡️

 - Own end-to-end logistics for hardware moving to and from the Fab Lab, including inbound receiving, inspection, and intake through outbound freight for customer deployments.

 - Manage carrier and freight relationships, schedule shipments, track deliveries and resolve delays, damaged goods and exceptions before they affect production timelines

 - Own inventory and asset accuracy across raw materials, components, spare parts, and finished hardware, including chain-of-custody documentation and deployment records

 - Coordinate with hardware and production teams to align build schedules with deployment timelines

 - Own day-to-day Fab Lab operations, keeping the workplace organized, functional, and safe, including layout, furniture, storage systems, and staging areas, ready to support production

 - Serve as the primary point of contact for facility vendors (cleaning, maintenance, utilities, security, waste management, and building management), and own maintenance, repairs, inspections, and preventative upkeep

 - Maintain facility documentation, vendor contracts, permits, and compliance records, and ensure adherence to California workplace and safety requirements, including identifying and escalating operational or compliance risks when necessary

 - Track facility, inventory, and logistics metrics and report operational health to leadership

 - Partner closely with Hardware, Engineering, Product, Story, and People Operations teams for onsite events, customer demonstrations, executive visits, and team gatherings

 - Operate effectively in a fast-moving environment with a high degree of ownership and autonomy

A Bit About You 🥇

Required:

 - 3+ years of experience in management of facilities, logistics, supply chain, operations, inventory, or related roles

 - Experience supporting hardware, manufacturing, lab, workshop, production, or warehouse environments strongly preferred

 - Expert knowledge of managing shipping, freight, inventory, or vendor operations via DHL, UPS, FedEx, USPS, et

 - Calm, low-ego, and highly collaborative, with a bias toward action and problem-solving

 - Knowledge of California workplace, safety, or facilities regulations

 - Highly proactive and detail-oriented with a strong sense of ownership

 - Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities simultaneously

 - Comfortable working hands-on in a physical environment, including moving and organizing equipment and materials (ability to lift 25+ lbs)

Nice to Have

 - Safety certifications relevant to facilities or industrial environments

 - Exposure to telecom, infrastructure, manufacturing, or industrial operations

Physical Requirements & Work Environment 🏭

The physical requirements described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job with or without reasonable accommo

source_jobs.title: Site Deployment Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Location: San Carlos, CA

We’re building Portals — custom communication pods deployed in libraries and public spaces to expand access to essential services. These private, technology-enabled spaces allow people in underserved communities to attend telehealth appointments, take exams, access government services, and connect securely online when they otherwise wouldn’t have a safe or reliable environment.

Once a Portal leaves our Fab Lab, this role helps ensure it arrives, installs, and operates successfully in the real world.

We’re looking for a Site Deployment Engineer to support the successful delivery and installation of Portals in customer environments alongside our Deployment & Field Operations Lead.

This role combines hands-on installation work, logistics support, and onsite technical execution. You’ll travel to deployment sites, assist with installation and system setup, troubleshoot issues, and help bring each Portal fully online before customer handoff. When not traveling to customer sites for installations, you will be based in the Fab Lab supporting assembly and build activities for Portals.

You’ll work closely with the Deployment & Field Operations Lead, fabrication teams, and customers to ensure deployments run smoothly.

This is a builder/operator role — ideal for someone who enjoys working with hardware, traveling to real-world environments, and solving problems hands-on.

About your role at Ready ⚡️

 - Travel to customer locations to assist with Portal delivery, unloading, assembly, and installation

 - Support mechanical setup, power-up, and system validation onsite

 - Configure and test networking, connectivity, and AV systems under guidance from the Deployment Lead

 - Troubleshoot hardware, connectivity, or installation issues during deployments

 - Help ensure each Portal is operational prior to customer handoff

 - Assist with shipment preparation and deployment readiness at the Fab Lab

 - Support coordination of installation logistics and site readiness

 - Work alongside local contractors or installation teams during deployments

 - Help maintain installation checklists, tools, and deployment equipment

 - Document deployment outcomes, issues, and lessons learned

 - Provide onsite support during customer training and handoff

 - Assist with post-install troubleshooting and field support needs

 - When not on deployments, support assembly, build, and integration work for Portals in the Fab Lab

A bit about you 🥇

 - Experience in field installation, AV deployment, hardware setup, technical operations, or similar hands-on work

 - Strong mechanical aptitude and comfort assembling physical systems

 - Ability to troubleshoot technical or hardware issues with guidance

 - Comfortable working in dynamic environments and solving problems onsite

 - Willingness to travel frequently for deployments (40%–60%)

 - Strong attention to detail and reliability during execution work

 - Comfortable interacting with customers and site staff

 - AV installation or low-voltage wiring experience

 - Basic networking or WiFi setup experience

 - Experience supporting hardware deployments or installations

 - Background in facilities support, construction assistance, or technical field work

 - Experience working alongside contractors or installation crews

Physical Requirements & Work Environment 🏭

The physical requirements described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job with or without reasonable accommodation.

Example:

 - Ability to perform repetitive tasks involving hands, wrists, and arms for extended periods.

 - Ability to stand and/or walk for extended periods during a work shift (up to 8 hours).

 - Ability to lift, carry, push, and pull materials and equipment weighing up to 25 lbs.

 - Ability to bend, stoop, kneel, crouch, and reach as required to perform assembly and inspection tasks.

 - Ability to use hand tools,

source_jobs.title: Technical Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
We are seeking a highly skilled and experienced Technical Product Manager to join our fast-paced and innovative team. As a Mid to Senior level professional with a strong technical background, you will play a pivotal role in shaping the direction of our products. Your ability to bridge the gap between technical development and business objectives, coupled with exceptional communication skills, will be instrumental in driving our product roadmap and delivering outstanding solutions to our customers.

About your role at Ready ⚡️

 - Define and communicate a clear product vision that aligns with business goals and market demands.

 - Develop and maintain a comprehensive product roadmap that outlines key milestones, timelines, and dependencies.

 - Continuously assess and adjust the roadmap to adapt to changing market conditions and customer feedback.

 - Leverage your technical background to deeply understand the complexities of our products, including architecture, development processes, and technical constraints.

 - Engage actively with engineering teams to evaluate technical feasibility and tradeoffs for proposed product features.

 - Translate technical constraints and engineering considerations into product decisions that stakeholders can understand and act on.

 - Work closely with cross-functional teams, including engineering, design, sales, marketing, and implementation teams, to ensure successful product development and delivery.

 - Act as a bridge between technical teams and stakeholders, effectively translating business needs into technical requirements.

 - Define clear and detailed product requirements, including user stories, use cases, and technical specifications.

 - Prioritize feature development based on customer feedback, market research, and business objectives.

 - Scope and evaluate AI-powered capabilities within our products; identify where AI can offer genuine user value and deliver product experiences that deliver that value in a dependable and transparent manner.

 - Exhibit exceptional communication skills, both written and verbal, to convey complex technical concepts in a clear and understandable manner.

 - Contribute to continuous improvement of our communication patterns and collaborative systems within the product team and across the organization.

 - Leverage AI tools for writing, analysis, and prototyping to produce shared sources of truth, synthesized insights, and perform more effective discovery.

 - Champion a user-centric approach to product development, ensuring that user needs and feedback drive product decisions.

 - Conduct user research and gather insights to inform product enhancements.

 - Engage directly with key customers to gather feedback, understand pain points, and identify opportunities for product improvements.

 - Build strong customer relationships to enhance our understanding of their evolving needs.

A bit about you 🥇

 - Bachelor's or Master's degree in a relevant technical field (e.g., Computer Science, Engineering) or equivalent experience.

 - Proven experience as a Technical Product Manager, ideally at the mid to senior level.

 - Strong technical background, with a deep understanding of software development processes and technologies. Prior background in software engineering, or hands-on experience with data modeling or technical requirements gathering is a significant plus.

 - Excellent communication skills, with the ability to convey complex concepts to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

 - Exceptional problem-solving and analytical skills.

 - Strong leadership and teamwork abilities.

 - Comfort working alongside engineering teams to understand architectural decisions and technical tradeoffs.

 - Experience leveraging AI tools to improve personal productivity and the quality of your work outputs.

 - Prior experience in a fast-paced, dynamic environment is preferred.

About Ready 🚀

 - Creative problem solvers approaching a legacy industry with a

notes: Mixed roles, with a focus on operations and product development. Some tech roles, but not dominant.
